class Solution {
public:
    bool isFascinating(int n) {
        int two_n = 2 * n;
        int three_n = 3 * n;
        
        // Check if 2n and 3n are 3-digit
        if (two_n < 100 || two_n > 999 || three_n < 100 || three_n > 999) {
            return false;
        }
        
        string s = to_string(n) + to_string(two_n) + to_string(three_n);
        
        if (s.size() != 9) {
            return false;
        }
        
        bool digits[10] = {false};
        
        for (char c : s) {
            int d = c - '0';
            if (d == 0) {
                return false;
            }
            if (digits[d]) {
                return false;
            }
            digits[d] = true;
        }
        
        return true;
    }
};

solution:
```class Solution {
public:
    bool isFascinating(int n) {
        int two_n = 2 * n;
        int three_n = 3 * n;
        
        if (two_n < 100 || two_n > 999 || three_n < 100 || three_n > 999) {
            return false;
        }
        
        string s = to_string(n) + to_string(two_n) + to_string(three_n);
        
        if (s.size() != 9) {
            return false;
        }
        
        bool digits[10] = {false};
        
        for (char c : s) {
            int d = c - '0';
            if (d == 0) {
                return false;
            }
            if (digits[d]) {
                return false;
            }
            digits[d] = true;
        }
        
        return true;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently checks the required conditions by first ensuring all numbers are 3-digit, then verifying the concatenated string contains exactly one of each digit from 1 to 9 with no zeros. The approach ensures optimal performance with constant time and space complexity.
